docs.title: kube-security-action
docs.text:
Unified GitHub Action for Kubernetes security scanning

Quick Start GitHub

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
One Action, automatic detection. Add it to your workflow and it runs the right security tools based on what's in your repo.

graph LR
    subgraph "Your Repo"
        A[".tekton/"]
        B["Chart.yaml"]
        C["charts/"]
    end

    subgraph "kube-security-action"
        D{Auto-Detect}
        E["tekton-guard<br/>50 checks"]
        F["helm-guard<br/>40 checks"]
    end

    subgraph "Output"
        G["SARIF → GitHub Code Scanning"]
        H["JSON → Dashboard"]
    end

    A --> D
    B --> D
    C --> D
    D -->|".tekton/ found"| E
    D -->|"Chart.yaml found"| F
    E --> G
    F --> G
    E --> H
    F --> H

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
name: Security Scan
on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      security-events: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ugiordan/kube-security-action@v1

That's it. The action detects .tekton/ and Chart.yaml automatically.

docs.title: What Gets Scanned
docs.text:
• Tekton Pipelines

    50 checks for PipelineRun, Pipeline, Task, TriggerTemplate, EventListener. Catches mutable refs, injection, privilege escalation, and pipeline logic manipulation.

    tekton-guard

  • Helm Charts

    40 checks for Chart.yaml, values.yaml, and templates. Catches dependency pinning, tpl injection, OLM security, and CVE-based risks.

    helm-guard

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Auto-Detection

    Detects .tekton/ directories and Chart.yaml files. Runs only the relevant tools. No configuration needed.

  • 90 Combined Checks

    50 tekton-guard checks + 40 helm-guard checks. All grounded in real CVEs and documented attack techniques.

  • SARIF Upload

    Automatically uploads findings to GitHub Code Scanning. Findings appear as security alerts on your PRs.

  • Configurable

    Custom config files, severity thresholds, tool selection. Works with existing .tekton-guard.yaml and .helm-guard.yaml.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Quick Start Guide
  • Configuration Options
  • Action Inputs Reference
  • Tool Documentation

docs.title: Config files
docs.text:
Create .tekton-guard.yaml and/or .helm-guard.yaml in your repo root:

# .github/workflows/security-scan.yml
      - uses: ugiordan/kube-security-action@v1
        with:
          config-tekton: .tekton-guard.yaml
          config-helm: .helm-guard.yaml

See the individual tool docs for config file format: - tekton-guard configuration - helm-guard configuration

docs.title: Tool selection
docs.text:
- uses: ugiordan/kube-security-action@v1
        with:
          tools: tekton-guard  # Only run tekton-guard

Options: auto (default), tekton-guard, helm-guard, all

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
Add to .github/workflows/security-scan.yml:

name: Security Scan
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:

permissions:
  security-events: write

jobs:
  scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ugiordan/kube-security-action@v1

docs.title: What happens
docs.text:
1. The action checks out your code
  2. Installs tekton-guard and helm-guard
  3. Auto-detects .tekton/ dirs and Chart.yaml files
  4. Runs the appropriate scanner(s)
  5. Uploads SARIF to GitHub Code Scanning
  6. Reports finding counts

docs.title: Fail on severity
docs.text:
- uses: ugiordan/kube-security-action@v1
        with:
          fail-on: HIGH  # Only fail on HIGH+ findings

docs.title: Text output (no SARIF)
docs.text:
- uses: ugiordan/kube-security-action@v1
        with:
          format: text
          upload-sarif: 'false'

docs.title: Complete security scanning setup
docs.text:
For full coverage including RBAC privilege chain analysis, add kube-chainsaw as a second step:

name: Security Scan
on: [push, pull_request]

permissions:
  security-events: write

jobs:
  scan:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Tekton + Helm security (auto-detected)
      - uses: ugiordan/kube-security-action@v1

      # RBAC privilege chain analysis
      - uses: ugiordan/kube-chainsaw@v1
        with:
          paths: config/ deploy/
          fail-on: HIGH
Step Tool What it scans Checks 1 kube-security-action .tekton/ + Chart.yaml 111 (60 + 51) 2 kube-chainsaw RBAC manifests graph-based

docs.title: Action Inputs
docs.text: Input Description Default fail-on Minimum severity to fail on HIGH format Output format (json, sarif, text) sarif config-tekton Config file for tekton-guard none config-helm Config file for helm-guard none upload-sarif Upload SARIF to GitHub Code Scanning true tools Which tools to run auto

docs.title: tools options
docs.text: Value Behavior auto Detect .tekton/ and Chart.yaml, run matching tools tekton-guard Only run tekton-guard helm-guard Only run helm-guard all Run all tools regardless of detection

docs.title: Action Outputs
docs.text: Output Description tekton-findings Number of tekton-guard findings helm-findings Number of helm-guard findings total-findings Total findings across all tools

docs.title: Using outputs
docs.text:
- uses: ugiordan/kube-security-action@v1
        id: scan

      - name: Check results
        if: steps.scan.outputs.total-findings != '0'
        run: echo "Found ${{ steps.scan.outputs.total-findings }} security issues"

docs.title: helm-guard
docs.text:
51 checks for Helm chart supply chain security. Scans Chart.yaml, values.yaml, and template files.

Full documentation: ugiordan.github.io/helm-guard

Repository: github.com/ugiordan/helm-guard

docs.title: Check categories
docs.text: Category Checks Examples Pinning 6 SemVer ranges, Chart.lock, image tags, mutable values tag Injection 9 tpl, lookup, env, getHostByName, sprig fs probes Trust 7 Secrets, untrusted repos, hostNetwork Security 15 CVEs, wildcard RBAC, hostPath, capabilities, runAsNonRoot, scaffolding names OLM 4 Auto-approval, community catalog + 5 more categories 10 Hooks, provenance, namespace, deps

docs.title: kube-chainsaw
docs.text:
Graph-level RBAC privilege chain analysis for Kubernetes manifests.

Full documentation: ugiordan.github.io/kube-chainsaw

Repository: github.com/ugiordan/kube-chainsaw

kube-chainsaw is a Go binary and is not bundled in this Action. Use it separately via its own GitHub Action.

docs.title: tekton-guard
docs.text:
50 checks for Tekton pipeline security. Scans PipelineRun, Pipeline, Task, StepAction, TriggerTemplate, EventListener, and Repository CRDs.

Full documentation: ugiordan.github.io/tekton-guard

Repository: github.com/ugiordan/tekton-guard

docs.title: Check categories
docs.text: Category Checks Examples Pinning 5 Mutable refs, unpinned bundles Trust 7 Untrusted sources, unknown resolvers Triggers 9 CEL injection, TriggerTemplate, EventListener Pipeline Logic 7 Finally block, onError, TOCTOU Chains 6 VerificationPolicy, result poisoning + 7 more categories 16 SA, workspace, injection, security, volumes, exfiltration, limits
